headline: Robinhood is a fully centralized custodian; all user crypto assets are under unilateral corporate control with no on-chain governance, timelock, or multisig requirement
short_headline: CEX: unilateral corporate custody, no on-chain checks

findings.text: Robinhood is a centralized exchange and broker-dealer (Robinhood Crypto LLC / Robinhood Financial LLC). There are no public smart-contract admin/owner addresses governing user funds — all custody is held by Robinhood's corporate entities under FINRA/SEC regulation. No 'owner', 'pendingAdmin', or 'governor' slot exists on a user-fund-holding contract to read, because the protocol is off-chain custodial. Known Robinhood Ethereum hot-wallet addresses (e.g. 0x0b722f A6ccbf9b9 and cluster) are plain EOAs or multi-purpose wallets, not governance proxies.

No proxy/upgrade mechanism exists for user-fund contracts because Robinhood does not publish or operate upgradeable on-chain contracts for custody. Their crypto infrastructure is entirely off-chain/custodial. The GitHub org 'robinhood' contains open-source utilities (robinhood/Hood, robinhood/pyfora, etc.) but no DeFi or custody smart-contract code. There is no proxy admin address to identify.

There is no on-chain execution path from 'vote passes' to 'implementation written.' All rule changes, fee changes, asset freezes, and fund movements are executed unilaterally by Robinhood Inc. employees through internal systems. The uncontested fast path delay is 0 seconds — Robinhood can freeze accounts, halt withdrawals, or move custodied assets with no on-chain delay or user-visible queue.

No public on-chain multisig governing user funds has been identified. Robinhood Crypto LLC holds user assets in omnibus accounts; internal controls are corporate (SOC 2, FINRA net-capital rules) rather than cryptographic. There is no emergency multisig, no Security Council equivalent, and no committee with public signer identities on a T1 path.

No on-chain governance exists. Robinhood has no governance token, no Governor contract, no Snapshot space, and no token-weighted voting. All protocol decisions (listing assets, changing fees, modifying withdrawal limits) are made by Robinhood Inc. corporate governance under SEC/FINRA regulatory oversight.

No separate emergency-pause smart contract exists. Robinhood can halt crypto trading and withdrawals unilaterally via internal platform controls — this has occurred historically (e.g., trading halts during the 2021 meme-stock events). There is no on-chain time-cap or guardian role; the pause power is held by Robinhood Inc. with no cryptographic constraint on duration.

Power tier: T1 (FUND-CRITICAL). Robinhood can freeze withdrawals, move custodied assets, change custody arrangements, or alter fee structures instantly with no on-chain constraint. The blast radius is maximum — all user crypto assets on all listed chains (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche) are held in Robinhood's custodial accounts and subject to unilateral corporate action. The fast path is: Robinhood employee/system acts → immediate effect, 0-second delay. This is T1 reachable with no timelock by a single corporate entity, meeting the 'red' grade criterion.

steelman.red: Robinhood Inc. holds all user crypto assets in omnibus custodial accounts with no on-chain governance, no timelock, no multisig requirement, and no cryptographic constraint preventing instant freezes, asset movement, or withdrawal halts — the definition of T1 reachable with zero delay by a single entity.
steelman.orange: Robinhood is subject to US regulatory oversight (SEC, FINRA, state money-transmission licenses), carries SIPC membership for securities, and operates under SOC 2 controls, providing off-chain institutional checks that are slower than but analogous to a multisig — one could argue this is 'orange' rather than 'red' because regulatory intervention provides a non-cryptographic brake on unilateral action.
steelman.green: Robinhood's regulatory status and public company accountability (NASDAQ: HOOD) create strong reputational and legal deterrents against misuse of custodial control, and their crypto assets are held at qualified custodians subject to NYDFS BitLicense and similar regimes — one could argue this is structurally safer than an anonymous 2-of-3 multisig.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because, under the rubric, T1 (fund-critical) power is reachable with no on-chain timelock by a single corporate entity (Robinhood Inc.), which maps directly to the 'red' criterion regardless of off-chain regulatory constraints. The Terms of Service explicitly grant Robinhood the right to restrict account access and halt transactions at its discretion. Regulatory oversight and public-company status are real protections but are off-chain, non-cryptographic, and cannot substitute for on-chain exit guarantees — they belong in the steelman for orange/green but do not meet the rubric's on-chain delay requirement. Evidence: Robinhood Customer Agreement grants unilateral halt/restriction powers; no on-chain governance contracts exist; the GitHub org contains no custody smart-contract code.

evidence.shows: Robinhood Customer Agreement granting Robinhood unilateral authority to restrict account access, halt transactions, and manage custodied assets at its discretion — no on-chain constraint or delay
Robinhood Crypto Customer Agreement establishing custodial (not self-custodial) relationship; Robinhood Crypto LLC holds assets on behalf of users with no on-chain governance
Robinhood GitHub org contains open-source utilities but no DeFi governance or custody smart-contract repositories, confirming no on-chain governance infrastructure
Placeholder block-explorer reference — Robinhood does not publish its custodial wallet addresses; no governance proxy admin address exists to inspect on Etherscan
Robinhood's public bug bounty program on HackerOne, confirming security contact channel
FINRA regulatory oversight of Robinhood Financial LLC — off-chain institutional control mechanism, not a cryptographic governance constraint
Robinhood Crypto custody model description — assets held in omnibus accounts by Robinhood Crypto LLC, not self-custodied by users

unknowns: C1: Robinhood does not publish its custodial wallet addresses or any governance contract addresses; no admin/owner slot readable on a block explorer for user-fund-holding contracts because custody is off-chain/corporate

C4: Internal corporate controls (who has signing authority over custodial accounts, what internal multisig or approval thresholds exist) are not publicly disclosed; no on-chain multisig address identified
C2: No proxy admin address exists to inspect; upgradeability is N/A for a pure CEX with no published on-chain custody contracts

protocol_metadata.about: Robinhood is a US-regulated retail brokerage and crypto exchange offering commission-free trading in equities, options, and cryptocurrencies via a mobile-first platform. Users hold crypto assets in custodial accounts managed by Robinhood Crypto LLC rather than in self-custodied wallets; Robinhood supports Bitcoin, Ethereum, and several other assets across multiple blockchains. The platform is distinctive for democratizing access to financial markets for retail investors, operating under SEC, FINRA, and state money-transmission regulatory frameworks. Robinhood (NASDAQ: HOOD) is a publicly traded company, not a DAO or token-governed protocol.
